author: codex-root

patch.value: pm-governance-audit dedupe-audit rejects --status all, breaking explicit all-lifecycle duplicate sweeps

Make the package-owned dedupe-audit command honor the same lifecycle-status filter contract as core list/search so agents can request duplicate analysis across every terminal and active status explicitly.

dedupe-audit accepts --status all as an explicit no-restriction sentinel; output echoes filters.status=all; configured custom statuses still validate; omission and --status all return the same all-item candidate set; help, package contracts, completions, docs, and focused tests stay aligned
The audit considers every lifecycle status, matching pm list/search --status all.
The command exits invalid_argument_value because its status domain excludes the all sentinel.
Agents cannot express the repository-required all-status duplicate sweep through the audit command and must know that omitting the filter has undocumented broader semantics.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check evidence: all-status searches for dedupe-audit status-all and governance audit contract parity found closed core parity item pm-i02t plus closed extraction lineage pm-vjk3/pm-79fr,but no active package-owned issue for the live pm 2026.7.13 rejection. Reproduced during the 1,784-item all-status audit.

message: Track live package audit filter-parity defect
